subsystems.name: Orchestrator / Entry Point
evidence: architecture-explorer\main.py imports 10 internal modules: git_miner, subsystem_detector, milestone_finder, source_validator, llm_generator, commit_analyzer, ownership_analyzer, temporal_analyzer, dependency_graph, heatmap_engine
main.py is the sole hub that fans out to every other module; no other module imports main.py
Uses sys, json, os for CLI/IO entry concerns

subsystems.name: Data Ingestion (Git Mining)
evidence: architecture-explorer\git_miner.py depends on subprocess and json — the only module shelling out to the external 'git' tool
Provides raw data consumed by downstream analyzers (referenced by main.py orchestration)
No inbound dependencies from other internal modules (leaf in the import graph)

subsystems.name: Source Validation
evidence: architecture-explorer\source_validator.py uses only os and stdlib — pure file-system validation concern
Listed among main.py's direct imports, executed early in the pipeline
source_validation.confirmed reports discovered root directories: architecture-explorer, lore_website

subsystems.name: Static Analysis (Dependency / Subsystem Detection)
evidence: architecture-explorer\dependency_graph.py imports ast and os — uniquely performs AST-based static analysis
architecture-explorer\subsystem_detector.py imports subprocess, os, collections — combines filesystem + git for clustering
Both modules are leaf-level analyzers with no outbound edges to other internal modules

subsystems.name: Commit / Ownership / Temporal Analytics
evidence: commit_analyzer.py: stdlib collections — commit metric computation
ownership_analyzer.py: stdlib collections — author/ownership aggregation
temporal_analyzer.py: datetime + collections — time-series analysis
All three share the same dependency profile (pure data transformation, no I/O, no subprocess), forming a cohesive analytical layer

subsystems.name: Milestone Discovery
evidence: milestone_finder.py imports re (regex) — text/pattern-based milestone extraction
Standalone leaf module with unique dependency footprint distinguishing it from numeric analyzers

subsystems.name: Output / Presentation
evidence: heatmap_engine.py: collections + os — risk-heatmap generation
mermaid_generator.py: json + os + sys + urllib.request — diagram emission
llm_generator.py: json + os + urllib.request + urllib.error — network-bound LLM-backed description generation
All three modules produce downstream artifacts (heatmap, mermaid, natural-language) and are sinks in the import graph

subsystems.name: Vendored Third-Party (KaTeX)
evidence: Five files under architecture-explorer\architecture-explorer\webview-ui\node_modules\katex\src\... exist in the dependency graph
These are build-time scripts for the KaTeX npm package (fontTools, parse_tfm), not part of the project's own logic
No internal module of architecture-explorer imports from these katex files; they are isolated in node_modules

patterns.name: Pipe-and-Filter / Pipeline
evidence: main.py acts as the single orchestrator sequentially invoking ingestion → analysis → output modules (10 direct imports)
Each non-orchestrator module is a leaf in the internal import graph (no inter-analyzer imports), enabling independent composability
Modules are cohesive by concern: mining, validating, analyzing, rendering

patterns.name: Layered Architecture
evidence: Layer 1 (Orchestration): main.py
Layer 2 (Ingestion): git_miner.py, source_validator.py — only modules interacting with the external world (subprocess, filesystem)
Layer 3 (Analysis): commit_analyzer, ownership_analyzer, temporal_analyzer, dependency_graph, subsystem_detector, milestone_finder — pure stdlib-based transformations
Layer 4 (Output): heatmap_engine, mermaid_generator, llm_generator — emit final artifacts, with llm_generator uniquely network-bound

patterns.name: Modular Monolith (Single-Process Tool)
evidence: All modules live under one top-level package 'architecture-explorer'
No inter-module imports among analyzer/output modules — strict separation enforced by the dependency graph edges
Single Python entry point (main.py) suggests a CLI tool rather than a long-running service

patterns.name: Fan-out Hub (Orchestrator Anti-pattern Risk)
evidence: main.py has 10 outbound edges to internal modules — a single point of high coupling
No façade or coordinator abstraction mediates between main.py and the analyzers

layers.name: Entry / Orchestration
evidence: architecture-explorer\main.py imports sys, json, os and 10 internal modules

layers.name: Data Ingestion
evidence: git_miner.py: subprocess, json
source_validator.py: os

layers.name: Analytical Core
evidence: commit_analyzer.py (collections), ownership_analyzer.py (collections), temporal_analyzer.py (datetime, collections), dependency_graph.py (ast, os), subsystem_detector.py (subprocess, os, collections), milestone_finder.py (re)

layers.name: Output / Rendering
evidence: heatmap_engine.py (collections, os), mermaid_generator.py (json, os, sys, urllib.request), llm_generator.py (json, os, urllib.request, urllib.error)

layers.name: External / Vendored (Out of Core Scope)
evidence: katex/src/fonts/generate_fonts.py, katex/src/metrics/extract_tfms.py, extract_ttfs.py, format_json.py, parse_tfm.py located under node_modules — third-party build scripts

riskClusters.name: Orchestrator Hub Coupling
riskClusters.description: main.py directly imports 10 internal modules with no façade. Any pipeline restructure requires editing the entry point, raising change-amplification risk.

riskClusters.name: Network-Bound Output Module
riskClusters.description: llm_generator.py and mermaid_generator.py use urllib for outbound HTTP. They are the only modules that can fail due to network conditions, making the pipeline non-deterministic and hard to test offline.

riskClusters.name: Vendored Third-Party Bloat
riskClusters.description: Five KaTeX node_modules .py files (font generation, TFM/TF metric extraction) appear in the analysis scope. These are not project logic and inflate the analyzed surface area; they should be excluded from architectural mining.

riskClusters.name: Empty Temporal / Commit / Ownership Data
riskClusters.description: commit_metrics, ownership_metrics, temporal_metrics, milestones, and commit history are all empty. Architecture is inferred solely from static dependency edges; evolutionary patterns (churn hotspots, ownership concentration, milestone boundaries) cannot be validated. Confidence is therefore bounded by static evidence only.

riskClusters.name: Subsystem / Milestone Data Sparsity
riskClusters.description: subsystems.subsystems, major_contributors, and milestones are empty lists. The detected subsystem boundaries above are derived strictly from import-graph cohesion, not from historical commit clustering.

recommendations.action: Introduce a Pipeline/Facade layer between main.py and the analyzers
recommendations.reason: main.py is a fan-out hub importing 10 modules directly; a coordinator class would isolate orchestration from the entry point and improve testability.

recommendations.action: Exclude node_modules (and other vendored third-party directories) from architectural analysis
recommendations.reason: KaTeX build scripts are not part of the project's own architecture and produce noise in the dependency graph.

recommendations.action: Inject an HTTP client abstraction for outbound calls
recommendations.reason: Direct urllib usage makes the pipeline non-deterministic and untestable offline; a swappable client enables offline/CI mode and retry policy.

recommendations.action: Add an interface/contract layer among analyzer modules (commit_analyzer, ownership_analyzer, temporal_analyzer, dependency_graph, subsystem_detector, milestone_finder)
recommendations.reason: Currently these modules are loosely coupled only by main.py's orchestration; formalizing a common input/output contract would prevent drift and enable parallel analyzer development.

recommendations.action: Capture and persist commit, ownership, and temporal metrics for the analyzed repositories
recommendations.reason: All four metric collections are empty in the supplied data; without these, the architecture model cannot leverage evolutionary evidence (churn, bus factor, milestone-driven refactors) and remains purely static.

recommendations.action: Surface structural validation as a hard gate before output rendering
recommendations.reason: source_validator currently runs alongside other modules without explicit ordering in the import graph; promoting it to a pre-render gate prevents generating diagrams/mermaid/LLM output for malformed or unparseable repositories.
